title: K8s Node Storage & Pod Lifecycle
description: Pod lifecycle tracking, node filesystem usage across all mount points, /var deep dive with per-pod ephemeral storage, container layers, and volume analysis.

panels.title: Running Pod Count Over Time
panels.description: Number of pods in Running phase over time for selected scope.

targets.expr: count((kube_pod_status_phase{namespace=~"$namespace", phase="Running"} == 1) and on(pod, namespace) kube_pod_info{namespace=~"$namespace", node=~"$node"})
count((kube_pod_status_phase{namespace=~"$namespace", phase="Pending"} == 1) and on(pod, namespace) kube_pod_info{namespace=~"$namespace", node=~"$node"})
count((kube_pod_status_phase{namespace=~"$namespace", phase="Failed"} == 1) and on(pod, namespace) kube_pod_info{namespace=~"$namespace", node=~"$node"})

panels.title: Pod Starts and Restarts Over Time
panels.description: Rate of pod container starts and restarts. Spikes indicate scaling events or crash loops.

panels.title: All Pods — Start Time, Phase, Uptime
panels.description: Every pod in scope with its start time, current phase, node, and uptime. Sorted by most recently started. Start time is a Unix timestamp converted to date.

panels.title: Recently Terminated Pods (Phase Changes)
panels.description: Tracks pods entering Succeeded or Failed phase. Useful for identifying workload completions and failures.

targets.expr: count((kube_pod_status_phase{namespace=~"$namespace", phase="Succeeded"} == 1) and on(pod, namespace) kube_pod_info{namespace=~"$namespace", node=~"$node"}) or vector(0)
count((kube_pod_status_phase{namespace=~"$namespace", phase="Failed"} == 1) and on(pod, namespace) kube_pod_info{namespace=~"$namespace", node=~"$node"}) or vector(0)
count((kube_pod_status_phase{namespace=~"$namespace", phase="Unknown"} == 1) and on(pod, namespace) kube_pod_info{namespace=~"$namespace", node=~"$node"}) or vector(0)

panels.title: Node Filesystem Overview — All Mount Points
Total Filesystem Size (all mounts)
panels.description: Sum of all filesystem sizes on selected node(s)

panels.title: Total Used (all mounts)
panels.description: Sum of used bytes across all filesystems on selected node(s)

panels.title: Total Available (all mounts)
panels.description: Sum of available bytes across all filesystems

panels.title: Overall Disk Utilization %
panels.description: Percentage of all non-tmpfs filesystem capacity that is used

panels.title: All Filesystems — Size, Used, Available, Usage %
panels.description: Every filesystem on selected node(s) with device info, mount point, total size, used, available, and usage percentage.

panels.title: Filesystem Usage % Over Time — All Mount Points
panels.description: Each line is a mount point. Shows how full each filesystem is getting over time. Helps identify filesystems approaching capacity.

panels.title: Filesystem Used Bytes Over Time — Per Mount Point
panels.description: Absolute bytes used on each filesystem over time. Each line = one mount point on the node.

panels.title: /var Deep Dive — Ephemeral Storage & Container Layers
/var Usage %
panels.description: How full /var is right now. /var is the primary ephemeral storage mount in Kubernetes — contains container images, logs, and ephemeral volumes.

panels.title: /var Capacity Breakdown
panels.description: Total size, used, and available on /var partitions

panels.title: /var Consumers — Category Breakdown
panels.description: Key consumers of /var space: container filesystem layers (ephemeral), container logs, and kubelet volumes.

panels.title: /var Usage % Over Time
panels.description: How /var utilization changes over time. Watch for upward trends that could lead to node eviction.

panels.title: /var Used Bytes Over Time
panels.description: Absolute bytes used on /var over time. Compare against total capacity.

panels.title: Per-Pod Ephemeral Storage Usage (Container FS on /var)
Per-Pod Ephemeral Storage Usage — Stacked
panels.description: Each color = a pod. Height = total ephemeral storage used by ALL pods. container_fs_usage_bytes measures the writable container layer, which lives on /var.

panels.title: Per-Pod Ephemeral Storage — Individual Lines
panels.description: Each line = one pod's ephemeral storage consumption. Compare against ephemeral limits to identify pods at risk of eviction.

panels.title: Top Pods by Ephemeral Storage Usage
panels.description: Pods ranked by current ephemeral filesystem usage. Shows usage, request, limit, and how much headroom remains before eviction risk.

targets.expr: sum by (pod, namespace, node) (container_fs_usage_bytes{namespace=~"$namespace", node=~"$node", container!=""})
sum by (pod, namespace, node) (kube_pod_container_resource_requests{namespace=~"$namespace", node=~"$node", resource=~"ephemeral.storage"})
sum by (pod, namespace, node) (kube_pod_container_resource_limits{namespace=~"$namespace", node=~"$node", resource=~"ephemeral.storage"})

panels.title: Container Writable Layer Usage — Stacked by Container
panels.description: Each container's writable overlay layer. This is the 'container layer' on /var that grows when containers write to the filesystem (not to a volume).

panels.title: Container Log Sizes Over Time
panels.description: Size of each container's log file tracked by kubelet. Large log files consume /var space. Uses kubelet_container_log_filesystem_used_bytes if available.

panels.title: Container Filesystem Write Rate
panels.description: Rate of bytes written by each container. High write rates on /var indicate containers that are actively growing their writable layer or ephemeral files.

panels.title: Container Filesystem Read Rate
panels.description: Rate of bytes read by each container from the filesystem.

panels.title: All Volumes — Capacity, Used, Available, Usage %
panels.description: PersistentVolumeClaims and EmptyDir volumes tracked by kubelet. These consume /var space when backed by the node's local filesystem.

panels.title: Volume Usage Over Time
panels.description: How volume usage changes over time. Each line = one PVC or EmptyDir.
